---
title: "Updating genomes and indices"
author: "AY"
---

This page collects the internal procedures for:

- updating genome FASTA/GTF sources
- rebuilding STAR indices
- switching the active STAR index used by mapping
- running mapping for single-end / paired-end projects

## When to update genomes / indices

Once every few years the genomic sequences must be updated. Also the STAR version for mapping progresses and should be updated when needed.

## Prerequisites / where to run

You can use any server as long as you can activate the conda environment with `snakemake`, `STAR`, `samtools`, `rseqc`, and `ucsc-wigtobigwig`.

### hpcl5002 (example)

```bash
cd /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/
conda activate GenomeIndexing
```

### ngs-vm (example)

Example conda environment creation:

```bash
conda create -n Mapping -c bioconda -c conda-forge snakemake-minimal star samtools rseqc ucsc-wigtobigwig
```

## 1) Update FASTA and GTF links

The config file used for genome indexing and mapping is `config.yaml` (in the pool-bcfngs folder). It contains a list of `fasta` and `gtf` links for each genome.

When a genome build is updated, update:

1. the `fasta` / `gtf` links
2. the organism prefix (folder name used for mapping), if needed

Example (D. melanogaster):

```yaml
Dme.BDGP6.22:
  fasta: "ftp://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/current_fasta/drosophila_melanogaster/dna/Drosophila_melanogaster.BDGP6.22.dna.toplevel.fa.gz"
  gtf: "ftp://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/current_gtf/drosophila_melanogaster/Drosophila_melanogaster.BDGP6.22.91.gtf.gz"
  SAindex: 12
```

to:

```yaml
Dme.BDGP6.28:
  fasta: "ftp://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/current_fasta/drosophila_melanogaster/dna/Drosophila_melanogaster.BDGP6.28.dna.toplevel.fa.gz"
  gtf: "ftp://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/current_gtf/drosophila_melanogaster/Drosophila_melanogaster.BDGP6.28.102.gtf.gz"
  SAindex: 12
```

## 2) Re-run genome indexing (STAR)

This step needs to be run only once (unless new genomes are added or indices are rebuilt for a new STAR version).

1. Log in to the server.
2. Activate the conda environment inside a `screen` session.
3. Edit the config file (example):

```bash
vi /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/scripts/config.yaml
```

4. Dry-run:

```bash
snakemake -nps ../scripts/getGenome_IndexGenome.Snakefile
```

5. Run for real (example with 12 cores):

```bash
snakemake -ps ../scripts/getGenome_IndexGenome.Snakefile -j 12
```

## 3) Switch the active STAR index (symlink approach)

Downstream mapping often expects the index at:

`/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/{organism}/starIndex/`

If you build STAR indices into versioned folders, keep the stable path by updating a symlink named `starIndex`.

Example loop (adjust STAR version folder name):

```bash
folders="Cel.WBcel235
Dme.BDGP6.28
Dps.3.0.49
Dre.GRCz11
Eco.HUSEC2011CHR1
Hsp.GRCh38
Mmu.GrCm38
Sce.R64-1-1"

for folder in $folders
do
  echo $folder
  cd ${folder}/
  rm starIndex
  ln -s starIndex2.7.10a/ starIndex
  cd ../
done
```

## 4) Mapping (per project)

Mapping is done with `STAR` for a specific genome in either paired-end or single-end mode.

### Change parameters before mapping

Adjust the project settings in the config file before running:

```yaml
project: "P152"
path: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P146/P152_ChIP_Asx_5_6/conc.fastq"
org: "Dme.BDGP6.22"
```

### Run mapping (dry-run first)

Change into the project folder containing the concatenated FASTQ files:

```bash
cd /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/
```

Dry-run then execute:

```bash
# single-end
snakemake -nps /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/scripts/Star.MappingQuant.SingleEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50

# paired-end
snakemake -nps /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/scripts/Star.MappingQuant.PairedEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
```

Remove `-n` to run for real.
